stories: U.S. risk-off flush: S&P 500 -1.74%, Nasdaq -2.38% — Nasdaq closes in “correction” territory
Geopolitics is the macro catalyst: U.S.-Iran “next steps” uncertainty is driving the risk premium
Oil back in the driver’s seat: higher crude tightens conditions and pressures duration/tech
Last few hours: Asia risk mood improves on de-escalation chatter; crude -5% and Asian equities higher
Oil volatility regime: the “up on conflict / down on talks” loop is now the market’s main intraday driver
Global equities are trading as one factor: geopolitics → oil → risk-on/off correlations
“Correction” optics matter: Nasdaq drawdown can mechanically change positioning behavior
Actionable index/ETF read-through: tech beta is the pressure point (Nasdaq -2.38% vs S&P -1.74%)
Oil shock reversal is the tradable fulcrum: Asia prints crude -5% on “talks” narrative
Wildcard: headline credibility risk—markets are now paying (and removing) a premium within hours
